mission.title: Install and Configure Langflow
mission.description: Langflow is a powerful platform for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows. It provides a visual authoring experience and built-in API servers, making it suitable for developers looking to integrate AI workflows into applications. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and resource management for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Langflow CLI tool to manage your Langflow deployments.

```bash

pip install langflow==1.8.0

```

> Use this command to install the specific version of Langflow.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Create a Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create langflow-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> This command creates a cluster named 'langflow-cluster'.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure kubectl to use the new cluster's credentials.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy Langflow
steps.description:
Deploy Langflow to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add langflow https://charts.langflow.org

helm install langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow --create-namespace

```

> Add the Langflow Helm repository.
> Install Langflow in the 'langflow' namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your Langflow deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment langflow --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace langflow

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Apply your monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Langflow
uninstall.description:
Remove Langflow from your cluster. This will delete all associated resources, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall langflow --namespace langflow

gcloud container clusters delete langflow-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command uninstalls Langflow from the specified namespace.
> This command deletes the entire cluster and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Langflow
upgrade.description:
Backup your current configuration, upgrade Langflow, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --namespace langflow -o yaml > langflow-backup.yaml

helm upgrade langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow

kubectl get pods --namespace langflow

```

> Backup current resources.
> Upgrade to the latest version of Langflow.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all --namespace langflow -o yaml > langflow-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the kubeconfig is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region=us-central1' to set up kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the Langflow deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the machine type in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur due to misconfiguration in the deployment or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace langflow' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Chart version not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Helm chart version may not exist in the repository.

**Fix:**
Verify the available chart versions with 'helm search repo langflow/langflow'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for multi-agent orchestration and improved observability features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods --namespace langflow' showing all pods in 'Running' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
